text:
COLLABORATION AGREEMENT  

THIS COLLABORATION AGREEMENT is made on  

BETWEEN: [REDACTED], a body corporate established pursuant to the [REDACTED] Act 1998 (Qld) of [REDACTED] (“[REDACTED]”)  

AND: [REDACTED] of [REDACTED] (“[REDACTED]”)  

AND: [REDACTED] of [REDACTED] (“[REDACTED]”)  

AND: [REDACTED] of [REDACTED] (“[REDACTED]”)  

AND: The University of Auckland, NZ of Private Bag 92019, Victoria Street West, Auckland 1142, New Zealand (“UA”)  

[REDACTED] and UA collectively called “the Participating Organisations”.  

RECITALS:  

A. [REDACTED] and the Participating Organisations have submitted a successful Application for a grant under the [REDACTED] Linkage scheme, to undertake the Project titled [REDACTED].  

B. This Agreement sets out the terms and conditions governing the implementation, conduct and outcomes of the Project and sets out the obligations of each Party.  

THE PARTIES AGREE AS FOLLOWS:  

1 DEFINITIONS  

Unless the context provides otherwise, the following words will have the following meanings:  

a) “Agreement” means this Agreement and includes any Schedules and/or Annexures.  

b) “Application” means the successful grant application referred to in Recital A, and included as Annexure A to this Agreement.  

c) “[REDACTED]” means the [REDACTED].  

[REDACTED]

```
Collaboration Agreement  
[Redacted]  
Page 2  

d) “Background IP” means IP pre-existing or independently owned or developed by or under the control of a Party which is reasonably required by a Party for the Project and unless specified in ITEM 8 of the Schedule as not included, includes any Improvements to such Background IP. Background IP known at the Commencement Date is listed in ITEM 8 of the Schedule.  

e) “Business Day” means (unless otherwise stated) each day except Saturday and Sundays and public holidays observed in Brisbane.  

f) “Chief Investigators” means the person or persons appointed to conduct the Project in accordance with clause 4.5 and 4.6.  

g) “Covered Personal Information” is Personal Information that is held or controlled by [Redacted] and includes any personal information that a Participating Organisation collects or handles in order to fulfill its obligations under this Agreement. [Redacted] has the legal right to determine what happens to Covered Personal Information.  

h) “Commencement Date” means the date specified in ITEM 2 of the Schedule.  

i) “Commercialisation” or “Commercialise” means to develop, manufacture, sell, hire or otherwise exploit for financial or other gain or advantage a product, process or information, or to provide a service, or to license, sub-license, joint venture or make any other similar arrangement with any third party to do any of those things.  

j) “Confidential Information” means any and all information, data and experience of a confidential nature relating to the Project, whether existing prior to the Commencement Date or created during the course of the Project whether scientific, technical, commercial, financial or other nature but will not include information that:  
   (i) is already in the public domain;  
   (ii) is received by a Party from an independent third party who is lawfully in possession and who is entitled to divulge it and is not under any obligation of confidentiality; or  
   (iii) becomes available to the public by any means other than breach of this Agreement by the Parties receiving the information.  

k) “Enrolling Institution” means the university at which the Student/s are enrolled.  

l) “Expiry Date” means the date specified in ITEM 2 of the Schedule.  

m) “Force Majeure” means anything outside the reasonable control of a Party, including but not limited to, acts of God, fire storm, earthquake, explosion, accident, war, rebellion, insurrection, sabotage, epidemic, pandemic, quarantine restrictions, labour dispute, labour shortage, transportation embargo, failure or delay in transportation, act or omission (including laws, regulations, disapprovals or failure to approve) of any government or government agency.  

n) “Grant Agreement” means the agreement of that name whereby the [Redacted] agrees to provide the Grant Funding to [Redacted], a copy of which is contained in Annexure B.  

[Redacted]  
```

```
Collaboration Agreement                                                                                     Page 3

o) “Grant Funding” means the grant provided by the [redacted] to [redacted] to conduct the Project in accordance with the Grant Agreement. The indicative Grant Funding at the time of entering into this Agreement is set out in ITEM 3 of the Schedule.

p) “[redacted] Contribution” means the financial and/or in-kind contribution of [redacted] as set out in ITEM 6 of the Schedule.

q) “GST” means any tax or increase in a tax imposed on the supply of goods and services imposed or assessed under A New Tax System (Goods and Services Tax) Act 1999 and all ancillary legislation.

r) “Improvements” means any improvements, variations, modifications, developments or adaptions made to the Background IP of a Party arising out of or developed in the course of the Project however it does not include any Intellectual Property that can be used or exercised without infringing the relevant Background IP.

s) “Insolvency Event” means any one or more of the following events occurring in relation to a party:
   (i) the party informs creditors generally that it is insolvent;
   (ii) the party being deemed to be, or stating that it is, unable to pay its debts as and when they fall due;
   (iii) the party has an administrator appointed;
   (iv) the party has a meeting of its creditors with a view to entering a scheme of arrangement or composition with creditors;
   (v) the party enters a scheme of arrangement or composition with creditors;
   (vi) the party has a receiver, receiver and manager or other controller appointed to any of its property or undertakings;
   (vii) the party resolves to wind up or has an application made to a court for its winding up or for the appointment of a provisional liquidator which is not stayed within ten (10) Business Days;
   (viii) a mortgagee of any of the party's property takes possession of any of that property;
   (ix) the party has a winding up order made in respect of it;
   (x) the party has execution levied against it by creditors, debenture holders or trustees or under a charge or other encumbrance; or
   (xi) the party as an individual becomes insolvent under administration as defined in section 9 of the Corporations Act 2001 (Cth).

t) “IP” includes copyright (including ‘Future Copyright’, as defined in section 10 of the Copyright Act 1968) and neighbouring rights, all rights in relation to inventions (including patent rights), plant varieties, registered and unregistered trademarks (including service marks), registered designs and circuit layouts, and all other rights resulting from intellectual activity in the industrial, scientific, literary or artistic fields.
```

```
Collaboration Agreement                                                                                     Page 4

u)  “Partner Investigator” means the person or persons appointed to conduct the Project in accordance with clause 4.5 and 4.6.

v)  “Participating Organisation Contribution” means the financial and/or in-kind contribution of each Participating Organisation set out in ITEM 6 of the Schedule.

w)  “Participating Organisation Entitlements” means entitlements afforded to a Participating Organisation under the Project, as detailed in ITEM 7 of the Schedule.

x)  “Party” means a party to this Agreement and "Parties" means all parties to this Agreement.

y)  “Project” means all of the research and development activity described in the Application and summarised in ITEM 1 of the Schedule.

z)  “Project IP” means all IP arising out of or developed during the course of the Project, except for Student IP and Improvements to Background IP.

aa) “Project Management Team” has the meaning as described in clause 4.8.

bb) “Reports” means the Project-related reports that [REDACTED] must supply the [REDACTED] in accordance with clause A5 of the Grant Agreement.

cc) “Student” means the student/s enrolled at the Enrolling Institution to undertake a research higher degree who will participate on the Project.

dd) “Student’s IP” means the copyright in a Student’s thesis prepared pursuant to their involvement on the Project.

2  INTERPRETATION

In the interpretation of this Agreement, unless the context provides otherwise:

a)  The expression “person” includes an individual, a body corporate, a joint venture, a trust, an agency or other body.

b)  Words in the singular include the plural (and vice versa) and words using a specific gender include all other genders.

c)  A reference to any document or agreement includes a reference to the document or agreement as validly amended, annotated, supplemented, varied or replaced from time to time.

d)  References to any legislation include any modification, re-enactment or replacement of the legislation or any legislative provision substituted for any legislation and statutory instruments issued under such legislation.

e)  All monetary amounts referred to in this Agreement are in Australian dollars.

f)  The headings are included for convenience only and do not affect interpretation.

3  TERM

3.1  This Agreement will commence on the Commencement Date and will continue (unless terminated earlier in accordance with this Agreement) until the Expiry Date. By signing this Agreement, the Parties each give their written consent for the Project to commence.
```

```
Collaboration Agreement                                                                                     Page 5

3.2 If any extension is granted by the [REDACTED] for the completion of the Project, then despite any other provision of this Agreement, the Expiry Date will be on the last day of the extension granted by the [REDACTED].

3.3 Where any inconsistency exists between:
    a) the Grant Agreement, including variations or changes to the Grant Agreement or the Project which are approved, mandated or directed by the [REDACTED];
    b) the Schedule;
    c) the other terms of this Agreement;
the provisions will take precedence in that order to the extent necessary to resolve the inconsistency.

4 THE PROJECT

Project Contributions

4.1 [REDACTED] and the Participating Organisations agree to collaborate and cooperate as reasonably required with each other, including in any dealings with any relevant government or regulatory authority, to complete the Project in accordance with their respective commitments in the Application, or as approved by the [REDACTED] through a formal variation request and this Agreement.

4.2 Provided that and for so long as the [REDACTED] continues to provide the Grant Funding to [REDACTED] for the Project, [REDACTED] will:
    a) administer the Grant Funding and the Participating Organisation Contributions in accordance with the Grant Agreement;
    b) provide the [REDACTED] Contribution; and
    c) provide the Participating Organisation Entitlements to the applicable Participating Organisation(s),
for the duration of the Project.

4.3 Provided that the [REDACTED] continues to provide the Grant Funding to [REDACTED] for the Project, the Participating Organisations will each provide their Participating Organisation Contribution for the duration of the Project.

4.4 Without derogating from each Party’s obligation to furnish its Participating Organisation Contribution, the Parties acknowledge that research work is of its nature uncertain and that particular outcomes and results from the Project cannot be guaranteed.

Chief Investigators, Partner Investigators and the Project Management Team

4.5 The Parties warrant that their specified personnel set forth in the Application are eligible to perform the Project in accordance with the Grant Agreement and have been approved to perform the Project. The Parties will each make those personnel available to the Project in the manner required by the Grant Agreement and will also ensure that those personnel comply with any applicable requirements of the Grant Agreement until the Expiry Date. If any of these persons cease to be employed by a Party, cease to remain eligible to participate in the Project or become unable to complete the Project, then that Party, subject to clause 4.6, may seek to appoint an alternative person who have the same or higher level of experience as the persons nominated in this clause.

[REDACTED]
```

```
Collaboration Agreement                                                                                     Page 6

4.6 Any changes to Chief Investigators or Partner Investigators under clause 4.5 requires the prior approval of the Parties and [REDACTED] approval under the Grant Agreement. The Parties must first agree to any changes in Chief Investigators or Partner Investigators, which can be formalised via an exchange of emails by the signatories to the agreement (or their delegate).

4.7 In a manner consistent with the requirements of the Grant Agreement, the Participating Organisations must provide a copy of this Agreement and the Grant Agreement (within a reasonable time after the execution of this Agreement) to all Chief Investigators and/or Partner Investigators employed or engaged by them and involved in the Project.

4.8 The Chief Investigators and the Partner Investigators will comprise the Project Management Team for the purposes of this Agreement. The Project Management Team will meet on a formal basis at least once a year to review the Project. Any Chief Investigator may call on the Project Management Team to meet at any time (provided reasonable notice is given), and such meetings may be held by telephone or via teleconferencing technology.

4.9 [REDACTED] and the Participating Organisations agree that they will each provide their respective Participating Organisation Contributions and undertake the Project diligently, competently and in a timely manner, with all due care and skill and in accordance with generally accepted professional, scientific, ethical, business and financial principles and standards (including the principles and standards mandated by the Grant Agreement).

Compliance with Grant Agreement

4.10 The Participating Organisations acknowledge that [REDACTED] has signed and is bound by the Grant Agreement. The Participating Organisations agree to not do anything that will or may cause [REDACTED] to be in breach of the Grant Agreement and agree to cooperate with [REDACTED] and provide any reasonable assistance which [REDACTED] may request in order to ensure compliance with the Grant Agreement. If there is any inconsistency between this Agreement and the Grant Agreement, the Grant Agreement will prevail to the extent of the inconsistency.

4.11 The Participating Organisations agree to comply with the applicable clauses within the Grant Agreement as if they were the Administering Organisation, including but not limited to clauses relating to specified personnel, conflict of interest, research ethics, handling of materials and data, privacy, eligible data breaches, child safety, fraud and the prompt reporting of fraud and compliance with legislation and policies. In particular, the Participating Organisations will promptly notify each of the other Parties of any actual or perceived conflict of interest arising in relation to the Project, consistent with the requirements of the Grant Agreement.

General

4.12 Each Participating Organisation will provide the information reasonably requested by [REDACTED] to enable [REDACTED] to prepare the Reports. The Participating Organisations acknowledge that [REDACTED] may disclose any information (including personal information) of the Participating Organisation to the extent [REDACTED] does so lawfully and in order to comply with the Grant Agreement.

4.13 Property in any plant, equipment, materials and other supplies purchased or otherwise acquired or constructed by [REDACTED] through the [REDACTED] Contribution or the Participating [REDACTED].
```

```
Collaboration Agreement  
[Redacted]  
Page 7  

Organisation Contributions will, unless otherwise agreed in writing or specified in the Application, vest in [Redacted].  

4.14 If this Agreement, or any supply made pursuant to this Agreement becomes subject to GST, and if the recipient of the consideration is liable to pay GST in relation to the supply, the Parties agree that the amount payable for the supply by any Party will be adjusted by the amount of the GST. Each Party agrees to do all things, including providing invoices or other documentation in such form and detail that may be necessary to enable or assist another Party to claim or verify any input tax credit, set off, rebate or refund in relation to any GST payable under this Agreement or in respect of any supply pursuant to this Agreement.  

5 INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY  

Background IP  

5.1 Each Party grants to each other Party a non-exclusive, royalty-free licence to use its Background IP for the purpose of carrying out the Project. Upon completion of the Project or in the event of early termination of this Agreement, [Redacted] will continue to have a perpetual licence to use each Participating Organisation's Background IP for university purposes including further research, teaching and academic publishing. If a Party specifies in ITEM 8 of the Schedule that any encumbrance applies to particular Background IP it makes available for the Project, the licence granted under this clause 5.1 to that Background IP is limited to the extent of the encumbrance.  

5.2 Nothing in this Agreement alters ownership of any Background IP of a Party and any Improvements to Background IP will vest in the owner of the Background IP upon creation.  

5.3 Each Party warrants that it is entitled to provide the licence of Background IP as required under clause 5.1.  

Project IP  

5.4 All Project IP will vest in [Redacted] upon creation and will be its sole property. Provided a Participating Organisation is not in breach of any provision of this Agreement, [Redacted] grants to each Participating Organisation a perpetual, non-exclusive, world-wide, royalty-free licence to use the Project IP for its own non-commercial purposes.  

The licence granted under this clause 5.4 does not permit any Participating Organisation to Commercialise the Project IP.  

Each Participating Organisation warrants that it has the necessary documentation (including assignments, consents and approvals) from all persons working on the Project on behalf of the Participating Organisation, to ensure [Redacted] will be:  
a) the sole owner of the Project IP under this Agreement; and  
b) able to comply with the requirements of the Grant Agreement.  

Student IP  

5.5 All Student IP will vest in the relevant Student upon creation and will be that Student's sole property.  

[Redacted]  
```

```
Collaboration Agreement  
[Redacted]  
[Redacted]  

Page 8  

6 INDEMNITY  

6.1 The Grant Agreement requires that each Participating Organisation provide certain assurances to [Redacted] in relation to the Project. Accordingly, each Participating Organisation confirms that:  
a) it will comply with the conditions of the Project; and  
b) the Participating Organisation complies with the Linkage Program eligibility criteria (which are published on the [Redacted] website).  

6.2 Each party (“Indemnifying Party”) indemnifies and agrees to keep indemnified the other parties, their respective directors, officers, employees and agents (“those Indemnified”) from and against any claim, action, damage, loss, liability, cost, charge, expense or payment however incurred or suffered by those Indemnified as a result of:  
a) any breach of the Grant Agreement, negligence or misfeasance occasioned by that the Indemnifying Party;  
b) an unlawful or negligent act or omission by the Indemnifying Party or any of its directors, officers, employees, agents or contractors in connection with performance of this Agreement; and  
c) any actual or alleged infringement of a Party’s or a third party’s IP rights by the Indemnifying Party or any of its directors, officers, employees, agents or contractors.  

6.3 The indemnities given in clause 6.2, will be reduced proportionately to the extent that the claim, action, damage, loss, liability, cost, charge, expense or payment was caused or contributed to by those Indemnified.  

6.4 Despite any other provision of this Agreement, the liability of a Party arising under and/or in connection with this Agreement shall exclude any liability for loss of profit, revenue, goodwill or business opportunities, damage to reputation and any indirect or consequential loss.  

7 PUBLICATION  

7.1 Subject to the confidentiality restrictions in clause 8, the Parties agree that [Redacted] may make Project-related publications at any time.  

7.2 The Participating Organisations may publish material relating to the Project, provided all material sought to be published is submitted for approval and approved by [Redacted] at least thirty (30) days prior to the intended publication (“review period”). Approval will not be unreasonably withheld nor delayed. [Redacted] will treat the proposed publication in confidence and may during the review period amend the proposed publication to remove any Confidential Information of [Redacted].  

7.3 All Project-related publications by any Party must acknowledge at a prominent place in the publication, the support of the [Redacted], the Chief Investigators and all Parties to this Agreement.  

7.4 Notwithstanding clause 7.3, no Party will use the name of another Party in any promotional material or advertising without the written permission of the other Party.  

8 CONFIDENTIALITY AND PRIVACY  

[Redacted]  
```

```
Collaboration Agreement  
[Redacted]  
Page 9  

Confidentiality  

8.1 Each Party may use Confidential Information of the other Parties only for the purposes of this Agreement and must keep confidential all Confidential Information of the Party disclosing it except for disclosures permitted under this Agreement and disclosures required by law.  

8.2 A Party receiving Confidential Information may disclose it to such of its officers, employees or contractors who have a need to know for the purpose of this Agreement provided that:  
   a) in the case of the recipient’s officers and employees, a direction has been given by the recipient to keep confidential all Confidential Information of the disclosing party; and  
   b) in the case of other persons approved in writing by the disclosing party, a confidentiality agreement has been entered into.  

8.3 Nothing in this Agreement will prevent the preparation, submission and examination of a Student’s thesis, provided that the Enrolling Institution will (where necessary to comply with confidentiality obligations) arrange for any examiners of the thesis to sign a confidentiality agreement which satisfies the provisions of this Agreement.  

8.4 The Enrolling Institution will use reasonable endeavours to ensure that each Student abides by confidentiality requirements with respect to any Participating Organisations’ Confidential Information.  

Privacy and Personal Information  

8.5 Each Party acknowledges that it will comply with the Privacy Act 1988 (Cth) (and/or the appropriate State privacy legislation) in the use, collection, storage or disclosure of any personal and/or health information collected or used during the Project. In relation to Covered Personal Information, each Participating Organisation:  
   a) assumes [Redacted] privacy obligations as if it were [Redacted];  
   b) may not use or disclose the Covered Personal Information for anything other than fulfilling its obligations under this Agreement unless it first received written approval from [Redacted];  
   c) must, when performing this Agreement, comply with Chapter 2 of the Information Privacy Act 2009 (Qld.) (“IP Act”) as if the Participating Organisation were an agency as defined by section 18 of the IP Act, and, after 1 July 2025, comply with the Queensland Privacy Principles (QPPs) and the IP Act s33 overseas disclosure rule and s41 requirement to comply with any QPP Code;  
   d) must immediately notify [Redacted] if it becomes aware of any unlawful use or disclosure or other breach of Covered Personal Information.  

9 TERMINATION  

9.1 This Agreement may be terminated at any time by mutual, written consent of the Parties.  

9.2 [Redacted] may terminate this Agreement without cause by giving at least 30 days notice in writing to the other Parties.  

9.3 [Redacted] may immediately terminate this Agreement by giving notice to the other Parties if the Commonwealth ceases to fund the Project pursuant to the Grant Agreement.  
```

```
Collaboration Agreement  
[Redacted]  
Page 10  

9.4 [Redacted] may immediately terminate the involvement of a Participating Organisation in the Project if:  
a) the Party commits a breach of this Agreement where that breach is not capable of remedy;  
b) the Party breaches any provision of this Agreement and fails to remedy the breach within 30 days after receiving a notice requiring it to do so; or  
c) an Insolvency Event occurs with respect to that Party.  

9.5 If this Agreement is terminated for any reason, [Redacted] must refund any unexpended and non-committed financial Participating Organisation Contributions to each applicable Participating Organisation.  

9.6 If a Party (“Affected Party”) is wholly or partially precluded from complying with its obligations under this Agreement by Force Majeure, then the Affected Party’s obligations to perform in accordance with this Agreement will (subject to the termination rights granted by this sub-clause) be suspended for the duration of the Force Majeure. As soon as practicable after an event of Force Majeure arises, the Affected Party must serve written notice (“Force Majeure Notice”) to the other Parties giving details of the Force Majeure event and the corresponding extent to which the Affected Party is unable to perform its obligations under this Agreement.  
a) The Affected Party may terminate its involvement in the Project by notice in writing to [Redacted] if a notified event of Force Majeure subsists for more than 90 days after the date of service of the corresponding Force Majeure Notice. [Redacted] may terminate an Affected Party’s involvement in the Project by notice in writing if a notified event of Force Majeure subsists for more than 90 days after the date of service of the corresponding Force Majeure Notice.  

9.7 Termination of this Agreement will be without prejudice to the rights of the terminating Party to seek damages for any breach from the Party in breach.  

10 SURVIVAL  

10.1 Clauses 4.13 (Property in Plant, Equipment and Supplies), 4.14 (GST), 5 (IP), 6 (Indemnity), 7 (Publication), 8 (Confidentiality and Privacy), 9 (Termination), 11 (Dispute Resolution) and 12 (General) will survive the Term of this Agreement.  

11 DISPUTE RESOLUTION  

11.1 Any dispute or difference arising out of or in connection with this Agreement (“Dispute”) will be resolved as follows:  
a) by agreement between the Parties to the Dispute through negotiations in good faith;  
b) if the Parties fail to resolve the dispute in accordance with clause 11.1a) the Parties will refer the Dispute to mediation by a mediator agreed by the Parties, or failing agreement, a mediator appointed by the President of the Queensland Law Society, on the terms of the standard mediation agreement approved by the Queensland Law Society. The reference will commence when any Party gives written notice to the other Parties specifying the Dispute and requiring resolution under this clause;  

[Redacted]  
```

```
Collaboration Agreement  
[Redacted]  
Page 11  

c) if the Dispute is not resolved within twenty-one days of the commencement of the reference under clause 11.1b) any Party may then, but not earlier, commence proceedings in any court of competent jurisdiction.  

11.2 Each Party will continue to perform this Agreement despite the existence of a Dispute or any proceedings under this clause.  

11.3 Nothing in this clause shall prevent a Party from seeking urgent interlocutory relief from a court of competent jurisdiction.  

12 GENERAL  

12.1 This Agreement will be governed by and construed in accordance with the law in force in Queensland, Australia. Each Party irrevocably and unconditionally submits to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the Courts of Queensland, and any courts which have jurisdiction to hear appeals from any of those courts and waives any right to object to any proceedings being brought in those courts.  

12.2 No variation or amendment to neither this Agreement nor the Project will be valid unless it is in writing and signed by all Parties.  

12.3 In the event of the invalidity of any part or provision of this Agreement, that part or provision will be severed from the valid parts and provisions and the invalidity will not affect the enforceability of any other part or provision of this Agreement.  

12.4 No rights under this Agreement will be deemed to be waived except where the waiver is in writing and is signed by the waiving Party. A waiver by any Party will not prejudice that Party's rights in respect of any subsequent breach of this Agreement by the other Parties. Any failure by a Party to enforce any clause of this Agreement or any forbearance, delay or indulgence granted by a Party to the others will not be construed as a waiver of the rights under this Agreement.  

12.5 No Party will assign, mortgage, charge or otherwise encumber their rights under this Agreement without first obtaining the other Parties’ written consent.  

12.6 A notice by any Party under this Agreement must be in writing:  
a) and delivered by hand or prepaid post to the address set out in ITEM 10 of the Schedule; or  
b) emailed in PDF version to the person nominated in ITEM 10 of the Schedule.  

A notice will be deemed to have been given and received if hand delivered on that day, if posted on the next Business Day in which the mail would ordinarily be delivered or if sent by email, immediately upon confirmation of a successful transmission.  

12.7 Nothing in this Agreement will be construed so as to constitute the Parties as partners or to create any agency or partnership between the Parties under any applicable law now or at any future time in force in the Commonwealth of Australia or the State of Queensland.  

12.8 No Party to this Agreement is empowered to incur obligations on behalf of the other Parties without the express consent of the other Parties.  

12.9 Each Party will pay its own costs of and incidental to this Agreement including legal costs.  
```

```  
Collaboration Agreement                                                                                      Page 12  
[Redacted]  

12.10  The provisions of this Agreement constitute the whole of the Agreement between the Parties.  

12.11  This Agreement may be signed in any number of counterparts (whether signed digitally,  
       electronically, or in original or scanned form) and all those counterparts together make one  
       instrument.  

12.12  A Participating Organisation must not subcontract the performance of all or any part of that  
       Participating Organisation’s obligations under this Agreement without the prior consent of  
       [Redacted], which shall not be unreasonably withheld. Each Participating Organisation is  
       responsible and liable for all acts and omissions of its subcontractors as if they were those of  
       the Party itself.  
```

```
clauses.text: g) “Covered Personal Information” is Personal Information that is held or controlled by [Redacted] and includes any personal information that a Participating Organisation collects or handles in order to fulfill its obligations under this Agreement. [Redacted] has the legal right to determine what happens to Covered Personal Information.

clauses.title: h) “Commencement Date” means the date specified in ITEM 2 of the Schedule.
clauses.text: h) “Commencement Date” means the date specified in ITEM 2 of the Schedule.

clauses.title: i) “Commercialisation” or “Commercialise” means to develop, manufacture,
clauses.text: i) “Commercialisation” or “Commercialise” means to develop, manufacture, sell, hire or otherwise exploit for financial or other gain or advantage a product, process or information, or to provide a service, or to license, sub-license, joint venture or make any other similar arrangement with any third party to do any of those things.

clauses.title: j) “Confidential Information” means any and all information, data and
clauses.text:
j) “Confidential Information” means any and all information, data and experience of a confidential nature relating to the Project, whether existing prior to the Commencement Date or created during the course of the Project whether scientific, technical, commercial, financial or other nature but will not include information that:  
   (i) is already in the public domain;  
   (ii) is received by a Party from an independent third party who is lawfully in possession and who is entitled to divulge it and is not under any obligation of confidentiality; or  
   (iii) becomes available to the public by any means other than breach of this Agreement by the Parties receiving the information.

clauses.title: k) “Enrolling Institution” means the university at which the Student/s are
clauses.text: k) “Enrolling Institution” means the university at which the Student/s are enrolled.

clauses.title: l) “Expiry Date” means the date specified in ITEM 2 of the Schedule.
clauses.text: l) “Expiry Date” means the date specified in ITEM 2 of the Schedule.

clauses.title: m) “Force Majeure” means anything outside the reasonable control of a Party,
clauses.text: m) “Force Majeure” means anything outside the reasonable control of a Party, including but not limited to, acts of God, fire storm, earthquake, explosion, accident, war, rebellion, insurrection, sabotage, epidemic, pandemic, quarantine restrictions, labour dispute, labour shortage, transportation embargo, failure or delay in transportation, act or omission (including laws, regulations, disapprovals or failure to approve) of any government or government agency.

clauses.title: n) “Grant Agreement” means the agreement of that name whereby the
clauses.text:
n) “Grant Agreement” means the agreement of that name whereby the [Redacted] agrees to provide the Grant Funding to [Redacted], a copy of which is contained in Annexure B.  

[Redacted]
```

```
clauses.text: q) “GST” means any tax or increase in a tax imposed on the supply of goods and services imposed or assessed under A New Tax System (Goods and Services Tax) Act 1999 and all ancillary legislation.

clauses.text: r) “Improvements” means any improvements, variations, modifications, developments or adaptions made to the Background IP of a Party arising out of or developed in the course of the Project however it does not include any Intellectual Property that can be used or exercised without infringing the relevant Background IP.

clauses.text:
```

```
clauses.text: v)  “Participating Organisation Contribution” means the financial and/or in-kind contribution of each Participating Organisation set out in ITEM 6 of the Schedule.

clauses.title: 2 INTERPRETATION
clauses.text: 2  INTERPRETATION

clauses.title: In the interpretation of this Agreement, unless the context provides
clauses.text: In the interpretation of this Agreement, unless the context provides otherwise:

clauses.title: a) The expression “person” includes an individual, a body corporate, a joint
clauses.text: a)  The expression “person” includes an individual, a body corporate, a joint venture, a trust, an agency or other body.

clauses.title: b) Words in the singular include the plural (and vice versa) and words using
clauses.text: b)  Words in the singular include the plural (and vice versa) and words using a specific gender include all other genders.

clauses.title: c) A reference to any document or agreement includes a reference to the
clauses.text: c)  A reference to any document or agreement includes a reference to the document or agreement as validly amended, annotated, supplemented, varied or replaced from time to time.

clauses.title: d) References to any legislation include any modification, re-enactment or
clauses.text: d)  References to any legislation include any modification, re-enactment or replacement of the legislation or any legislative provision substituted for any legislation and statutory instruments issued under such legislation.

clauses.title: e) All monetary amounts referred to in this Agreement are in Australian
clauses.text: e)  All monetary amounts referred to in this Agreement are in Australian dollars.

clauses.title: f) The headings are included for convenience only and do not affect
clauses.text: f)  The headings are included for convenience only and do not affect interpretation.

clauses.title: 3 TERM
clauses.text: 3  TERM

clauses.title: 3.1 This Agreement will commence on the Commencement Date and will
clauses.text:
3.1  This Agreement will commence on the Commencement Date and will continue (unless terminated earlier in accordance with this Agreement) until the Expiry Date. By signing this Agreement, the Parties each give their written consent for the Project to commence.
```

```
clauses.title: 4.1 [REDACTED] and the Participating Organisations agree to collaborate and
clauses.text: 4.1 [REDACTED] and the Participating Organisations agree to collaborate and cooperate as reasonably required with each other, including in any dealings with any relevant government or regulatory authority, to complete the Project in accordance with their respective commitments in the Application, or as approved by the [REDACTED] through a formal variation request and this Agreement.

clauses.title: 4.2 Provided that and for so long as the [REDACTED] continues to provide the
clauses.text:
4.2 Provided that and for so long as the [REDACTED] continues to provide the Grant Funding to [REDACTED] for the Project, [REDACTED] will:
    a) administer the Grant Funding and the Participating Organisation Contributions in accordance with the Grant Agreement;
    b) provide the [REDACTED] Contribution; and
    c) provide the Participating Organisation Entitlements to the applicable Participating Organisation(s),
for the duration of the Project.

clauses.title: 4.3 Provided that the [REDACTED] continues to provide the Grant Funding to
clauses.text: 4.3 Provided that the [REDACTED] continues to provide the Grant Funding to [REDACTED] for the Project, the Participating Organisations will each provide their Participating Organisation Contribution for the duration of the Project.

clauses.title: 4.4 Without derogating from each Party’s obligation to furnish its
clauses.text: 4.4 Without derogating from each Party’s obligation to furnish its Participating Organisation Contribution, the Parties acknowledge that research work is of its nature uncertain and that particular outcomes and results from the Project cannot be guaranteed.

clauses.title: Chief Investigators, Partner Investigators and the Project Management Team
clauses.text: Chief Investigators, Partner Investigators and the Project Management Team

clauses.title: 4.5 The Parties warrant that their specified personnel set forth in the
clauses.text:
4.5 The Parties warrant that their specified personnel set forth in the Application are eligible to perform the Project in accordance with the Grant Agreement and have been approved to perform the Project. The Parties will each make those personnel available to the Project in the manner required by the Grant Agreement and will also ensure that those personnel comply with any applicable requirements of the Grant Agreement until the Expiry Date. If any of these persons cease to be employed by a Party, cease to remain eligible to participate in the Project or become unable to complete the Project, then that Party, subject to clause 4.6, may seek to appoint an alternative person who have the same or higher level of experience as the persons nominated in this clause.

[REDACTED]
```

```
Collaboration Agreement                                                                                     Page 6

clauses.title: 4.6 Any changes to Chief Investigators or Partner Investigators under clause
clauses.text: 4.6 Any changes to Chief Investigators or Partner Investigators under clause 4.5 requires the prior approval of the Parties and [REDACTED] approval under the Grant Agreement. The Parties must first agree to any changes in Chief Investigators or Partner Investigators, which can be formalised via an exchange of emails by the signatories to the agreement (or their delegate).

clauses.title: 4.7 In a manner consistent with the requirements of the Grant Agreement,
clauses.text: 4.7 In a manner consistent with the requirements of the Grant Agreement, the Participating Organisations must provide a copy of this Agreement and the Grant Agreement (within a reasonable time after the execution of this Agreement) to all Chief Investigators and/or Partner Investigators employed or engaged by them and involved in the Project.

clauses.title: 4.8 The Chief Investigators and the Partner Investigators will comprise the
clauses.text: 4.8 The Chief Investigators and the Partner Investigators will comprise the Project Management Team for the purposes of this Agreement. The Project Management Team will meet on a formal basis at least once a year to review the Project. Any Chief Investigator may call on the Project Management Team to meet at any time (provided reasonable notice is given), and such meetings may be held by telephone or via teleconferencing technology.

clauses.title: 4.9 [REDACTED] and the Participating Organisations agree that they will each
clauses.text: 4.9 [REDACTED] and the Participating Organisations agree that they will each provide their respective Participating Organisation Contributions and undertake the Project diligently, competently and in a timely manner, with all due care and skill and in accordance with generally accepted professional, scientific, ethical, business and financial principles and standards (including the principles and standards mandated by the Grant Agreement).

clauses.title: Compliance with Grant Agreement
clauses.text: Compliance with Grant Agreement

clauses.title: 4.10 The Participating Organisations acknowledge that [REDACTED] has signed
clauses.text: 4.10 The Participating Organisations acknowledge that [REDACTED] has signed and is bound by the Grant Agreement. The Participating Organisations agree to not do anything that will or may cause [REDACTED] to be in breach of the Grant Agreement and agree to cooperate with [REDACTED] and provide any reasonable assistance which [REDACTED] may request in order to ensure compliance with the Grant Agreement. If there is any inconsistency between this Agreement and the Grant Agreement, the Grant Agreement will prevail to the extent of the inconsistency.

clauses.title: 4.11 The Participating Organisations agree to comply with the applicable
clauses.text: 4.11 The Participating Organisations agree to comply with the applicable clauses within the Grant Agreement as if they were the Administering Organisation, including but not limited to clauses relating to specified personnel, conflict of interest, research ethics, handling of materials and data, privacy, eligible data breaches, child safety, fraud and the prompt reporting of fraud and compliance with legislation and policies. In particular, the Participating Organisations will promptly notify each of the other Parties of any actual or perceived conflict of interest arising in relation to the Project, consistent with the requirements of the Grant Agreement.

clauses.title: General
clauses.text: General

clauses.title: 4.12 Each Participating Organisation will provide the information reasonably
clauses.text: 4.12 Each Participating Organisation will provide the information reasonably requested by [REDACTED] to enable [REDACTED] to prepare the Reports. The Participating Organisations acknowledge that [REDACTED] may disclose any information (including personal information) of the Participating Organisation to the extent [REDACTED] does so lawfully and in order to comply with the Grant Agreement.

clauses.text:
4.13 Property in any plant, equipment, materials and other supplies purchased or otherwise acquired or constructed by [REDACTED] through the [REDACTED] Contribution or the Participating [REDACTED].
```

```
clauses.title: 4.14 If this Agreement, or any supply made pursuant to this Agreement
clauses.text: 4.14 If this Agreement, or any supply made pursuant to this Agreement becomes subject to GST, and if the recipient of the consideration is liable to pay GST in relation to the supply, the Parties agree that the amount payable for the supply by any Party will be adjusted by the amount of the GST. Each Party agrees to do all things, including providing invoices or other documentation in such form and detail that may be necessary to enable or assist another Party to claim or verify any input tax credit, set off, rebate or refund in relation to any GST payable under this Agreement or in respect of any supply pursuant to this Agreement.

clauses.title: 5 INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
clauses.text:
5 INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY  

Background IP

clauses.title: 5.1 Each Party grants to each other Party a non-exclusive, royalty-free
clauses.text: 5.1 Each Party grants to each other Party a non-exclusive, royalty-free licence to use its Background IP for the purpose of carrying out the Project. Upon completion of the Project or in the event of early termination of this Agreement, [Redacted] will continue to have a perpetual licence to use each Participating Organisation's Background IP for university purposes including further research, teaching and academic publishing. If a Party specifies in ITEM 8 of the Schedule that any encumbrance applies to particular Background IP it makes available for the Project, the licence granted under this clause 5.1 to that Background IP is limited to the extent of the encumbrance.

clauses.title: 5.2 Nothing in this Agreement alters ownership of any Background IP of a
clauses.text: 5.2 Nothing in this Agreement alters ownership of any Background IP of a Party and any Improvements to Background IP will vest in the owner of the Background IP upon creation.

clauses.title: 5.3 Each Party warrants that it is entitled to provide the licence of
clauses.text:
5.3 Each Party warrants that it is entitled to provide the licence of Background IP as required under clause 5.1.  

Project IP

clauses.title: 5.4 All Project IP will vest in [Redacted] upon creation and will be its
clauses.text:
5.4 All Project IP will vest in [Redacted] upon creation and will be its sole property. Provided a Participating Organisation is not in breach of any provision of this Agreement, [Redacted] grants to each Participating Organisation a perpetual, non-exclusive, world-wide, royalty-free licence to use the Project IP for its own non-commercial purposes.  

The licence granted under this clause 5.4 does not permit any Participating Organisation to Commercialise the Project IP.  

Each Participating Organisation warrants that it has the necessary documentation (including assignments, consents and approvals) from all persons working on the Project on behalf of the Participating Organisation, to ensure [Redacted] will be:  
a) the sole owner of the Project IP under this Agreement; and  
b) able to comply with the requirements of the Grant Agreement.  

Student IP

clauses.title: 5.5 All Student IP will vest in the relevant Student upon creation and will
clauses.text:
5.5 All Student IP will vest in the relevant Student upon creation and will be that Student's sole property.  

[Redacted]
```

```
clauses.title: 6 INDEMNITY
clauses.text: 6 INDEMNITY

clauses.title: 6.1 The Grant Agreement requires that each Participating Organisation
clauses.text:
6.1 The Grant Agreement requires that each Participating Organisation provide certain assurances to [Redacted] in relation to the Project. Accordingly, each Participating Organisation confirms that:  
a) it will comply with the conditions of the Project; and  
b) the Participating Organisation complies with the Linkage Program eligibility criteria (which are published on the [Redacted] website).

clauses.title: 6.2 Each party (“Indemnifying Party”) indemnifies and agrees to keep
clauses.text:
6.2 Each party (“Indemnifying Party”) indemnifies and agrees to keep indemnified the other parties, their respective directors, officers, employees and agents (“those Indemnified”) from and against any claim, action, damage, loss, liability, cost, charge, expense or payment however incurred or suffered by those Indemnified as a result of:  
a) any breach of the Grant Agreement, negligence or misfeasance occasioned by that the Indemnifying Party;  
b) an unlawful or negligent act or omission by the Indemnifying Party or any of its directors, officers, employees, agents or contractors in connection with performance of this Agreement; and  
c) any actual or alleged infringement of a Party’s or a third party’s IP rights by the Indemnifying Party or any of its directors, officers, employees, agents or contractors.

clauses.title: 6.3 The indemnities given in clause 6.2, will be reduced proportionately to
clauses.text: 6.3 The indemnities given in clause 6.2, will be reduced proportionately to the extent that the claim, action, damage, loss, liability, cost, charge, expense or payment was caused or contributed to by those Indemnified.

clauses.title: 6.4 Despite any other provision of this Agreement, the liability of a Party
clauses.text: 6.4 Despite any other provision of this Agreement, the liability of a Party arising under and/or in connection with this Agreement shall exclude any liability for loss of profit, revenue, goodwill or business opportunities, damage to reputation and any indirect or consequential loss.

clauses.title: 7 PUBLICATION
clauses.text: 7 PUBLICATION

clauses.title: 7.1 Subject to the confidentiality restrictions in clause 8, the Parties
clauses.text: 7.1 Subject to the confidentiality restrictions in clause 8, the Parties agree that [Redacted] may make Project-related publications at any time.

clauses.title: 7.2 The Participating Organisations may publish material relating to the
clauses.text: 7.2 The Participating Organisations may publish material relating to the Project, provided all material sought to be published is submitted for approval and approved by [Redacted] at least thirty (30) days prior to the intended publication (“review period”). Approval will not be unreasonably withheld nor delayed. [Redacted] will treat the proposed publication in confidence and may during the review period amend the proposed publication to remove any Confidential Information of [Redacted].

clauses.title: 7.3 All Project-related publications by any Party must acknowledge at a
clauses.text: 7.3 All Project-related publications by any Party must acknowledge at a prominent place in the publication, the support of the [Redacted], the Chief Investigators and all Parties to this Agreement.

clauses.title: 7.4 Notwithstanding clause 7.3, no Party will use the name of another Party
clauses.text: 7.4 Notwithstanding clause 7.3, no Party will use the name of another Party in any promotional material or advertising without the written permission of the other Party.
```

```
clauses.title: Confidentiality
clauses.text: Confidentiality

clauses.title: 8.1 Each Party may use Confidential Information of the other Parties only
clauses.text: 8.1 Each Party may use Confidential Information of the other Parties only for the purposes of this Agreement and must keep confidential all Confidential Information of the Party disclosing it except for disclosures permitted under this Agreement and disclosures required by law.

clauses.title: 8.2 A Party receiving Confidential Information may disclose it to such of
clauses.text:
8.2 A Party receiving Confidential Information may disclose it to such of its officers, employees or contractors who have a need to know for the purpose of this Agreement provided that:  
   a) in the case of the recipient’s officers and employees, a direction has been given by the recipient to keep confidential all Confidential Information of the disclosing party; and  
   b) in the case of other persons approved in writing by the disclosing party, a confidentiality agreement has been entered into.

clauses.title: 8.3 Nothing in this Agreement will prevent the preparation, submission and
clauses.text: 8.3 Nothing in this Agreement will prevent the preparation, submission and examination of a Student’s thesis, provided that the Enrolling Institution will (where necessary to comply with confidentiality obligations) arrange for any examiners of the thesis to sign a confidentiality agreement which satisfies the provisions of this Agreement.

clauses.title: 8.4 The Enrolling Institution will use reasonable endeavours to ensure that
clauses.text: 8.4 The Enrolling Institution will use reasonable endeavours to ensure that each Student abides by confidentiality requirements with respect to any Participating Organisations’ Confidential Information.

clauses.text: Privacy and Personal Information

clauses.text: 9.1 This Agreement may be terminated at any time by mutual, written consent of the Parties.

clauses.title: 9.2 [Redacted] may terminate this Agreement without cause by giving at least
clauses.text: 9.2 [Redacted] may terminate this Agreement without cause by giving at least 30 days notice in writing to the other Parties.

clauses.text:
9.3 [Redacted] may immediately terminate this Agreement by giving notice to the other Parties if the Commonwealth ceases to fund the Project pursuant to the Grant Agreement.
```

```
clauses.title: 9.4 [Redacted] may immediately terminate the involvement of a Participating
clauses.text:
9.4 [Redacted] may immediately terminate the involvement of a Participating Organisation in the Project if:  
a) the Party commits a breach of this Agreement where that breach is not capable of remedy;  
b) the Party breaches any provision of this Agreement and fails to remedy the breach within 30 days after receiving a notice requiring it to do so; or  
c) an Insolvency Event occurs with respect to that Party.

clauses.title: 9.5 If this Agreement is terminated for any reason, [Redacted] must refund
clauses.text: 9.5 If this Agreement is terminated for any reason, [Redacted] must refund any unexpended and non-committed financial Participating Organisation Contributions to each applicable Participating Organisation.

clauses.title: 9.6 If a Party (“Affected Party”) is wholly or partially precluded from
clauses.text:
9.6 If a Party (“Affected Party”) is wholly or partially precluded from complying with its obligations under this Agreement by Force Majeure, then the Affected Party’s obligations to perform in accordance with this Agreement will (subject to the termination rights granted by this sub-clause) be suspended for the duration of the Force Majeure. As soon as practicable after an event of Force Majeure arises, the Affected Party must serve written notice (“Force Majeure Notice”) to the other Parties giving details of the Force Majeure event and the corresponding extent to which the Affected Party is unable to perform its obligations under this Agreement.  
a) The Affected Party may terminate its involvement in the Project by notice in writing to [Redacted] if a notified event of Force Majeure subsists for more than 90 days after the date of service of the corresponding Force Majeure Notice. [Redacted] may terminate an Affected Party’s involvement in the Project by notice in writing if a notified event of Force Majeure subsists for more than 90 days after the date of service of the corresponding Force Majeure Notice.

clauses.title: 9.7 Termination of this Agreement will be without prejudice to the rights
clauses.text: 9.7 Termination of this Agreement will be without prejudice to the rights of the terminating Party to seek damages for any breach from the Party in breach.

clauses.title: 10 SURVIVAL
clauses.text: 10 SURVIVAL

clauses.title: 10.1 Clauses 4.13 (Property in Plant, Equipment and Supplies), 4.14 (GST),
clauses.text: 10.1 Clauses 4.13 (Property in Plant, Equipment and Supplies), 4.14 (GST), 5 (IP), 6 (Indemnity), 7 (Publication), 8 (Confidentiality and Privacy), 9 (Termination), 11 (Dispute Resolution) and 12 (General) will survive the Term of this Agreement.

clauses.title: 11 DISPUTE RESOLUTION
clauses.text: 11 DISPUTE RESOLUTION

clauses.title: 11.1 Any dispute or difference arising out of or in connection with this
clauses.text:
11.1 Any dispute or difference arising out of or in connection with this Agreement (“Dispute”) will be resolved as follows:  
a) by agreement between the Parties to the Dispute through negotiations in good faith;  
b) if the Parties fail to resolve the dispute in accordance with clause 11.1a) the Parties will refer the Dispute to mediation by a mediator agreed by the Parties, or failing agreement, a mediator appointed by the President of the Queensland Law Society, on the terms of the standard mediation agreement approved by the Queensland Law Society. The reference will commence when any Party gives written notice to the other Parties specifying the Dispute and requiring resolution under this clause;  

[Redacted]  
```

```
Collaboration Agreement  
[Redacted]  
Page 11  

c) if the Dispute is not resolved within twenty-one days of the commencement of the reference under clause 11.1b) any Party may then, but not earlier, commence proceedings in any court of competent jurisdiction.

clauses.title: 11.2 Each Party will continue to perform this Agreement despite the
clauses.text: 11.2 Each Party will continue to perform this Agreement despite the existence of a Dispute or any proceedings under this clause.

clauses.title: 11.3 Nothing in this clause shall prevent a Party from seeking urgent
clauses.text: 11.3 Nothing in this clause shall prevent a Party from seeking urgent interlocutory relief from a court of competent jurisdiction.

clauses.title: 12 GENERAL
clauses.text: 12 GENERAL

clauses.title: 12.1 This Agreement will be governed by and construed in accordance with
clauses.text: 12.1 This Agreement will be governed by and construed in accordance with the law in force in Queensland, Australia. Each Party irrevocably and unconditionally submits to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the Courts of Queensland, and any courts which have jurisdiction to hear appeals from any of those courts and waives any right to object to any proceedings being brought in those courts.

clauses.title: 12.2 No variation or amendment to neither this Agreement nor the Project
clauses.text: 12.2 No variation or amendment to neither this Agreement nor the Project will be valid unless it is in writing and signed by all Parties.

clauses.title: 12.3 In the event of the invalidity of any part or provision of this
clauses.text: 12.3 In the event of the invalidity of any part or provision of this Agreement, that part or provision will be severed from the valid parts and provisions and the invalidity will not affect the enforceability of any other part or provision of this Agreement.

clauses.title: 12.4 No rights under this Agreement will be deemed to be waived except
clauses.text: 12.4 No rights under this Agreement will be deemed to be waived except where the waiver is in writing and is signed by the waiving Party. A waiver by any Party will not prejudice that Party's rights in respect of any subsequent breach of this Agreement by the other Parties. Any failure by a Party to enforce any clause of this Agreement or any forbearance, delay or indulgence granted by a Party to the others will not be construed as a waiver of the rights under this Agreement.

clauses.title: 12.5 No Party will assign, mortgage, charge or otherwise encumber their
clauses.text: 12.5 No Party will assign, mortgage, charge or otherwise encumber their rights under this Agreement without first obtaining the other Parties’ written consent.

clauses.title: 12.6 A notice by any Party under this Agreement must be in writing:
clauses.text:
12.6 A notice by any Party under this Agreement must be in writing:  
a) and delivered by hand or prepaid post to the address set out in ITEM 10 of the Schedule; or  
b) emailed in PDF version to the person nominated in ITEM 10 of the Schedule.  

A notice will be deemed to have been given and received if hand delivered on that day, if posted on the next Business Day in which the mail would ordinarily be delivered or if sent by email, immediately upon confirmation of a successful transmission.

clauses.title: 12.7 Nothing in this Agreement will be construed so as to constitute the
clauses.text: 12.7 Nothing in this Agreement will be construed so as to constitute the Parties as partners or to create any agency or partnership between the Parties under any applicable law now or at any future time in force in the Commonwealth of Australia or the State of Queensland.

clauses.title: 12.8 No Party to this Agreement is empowered to incur obligations on behalf
clauses.text: 12.8 No Party to this Agreement is empowered to incur obligations on behalf of the other Parties without the express consent of the other Parties.

clauses.title: 12.9 Each Party will pay its own costs of and incidental to this Agreement
clauses.text:
12.9 Each Party will pay its own costs of and incidental to this Agreement including legal costs.  
```

```  
clauses.title: 12.10 The provisions of this Agreement constitute the whole of the
clauses.text: 12.10  The provisions of this Agreement constitute the whole of the Agreement between the Parties.

clauses.title: 12.11 This Agreement may be signed in any number of counterparts (whether
clauses.text:
12.11  This Agreement may be signed in any number of counterparts (whether signed digitally,  
       electronically, or in original or scanned form) and all those counterparts together make one  
       instrument.

clauses.title: 12.12 A Participating Organisation must not subcontract the performance of
clauses.text:
12.12  A Participating Organisation must not subcontract the performance of all or any part of that  
       Participating Organisation’s obligations under this Agreement without the prior consent of  
       [Redacted], which shall not be unreasonably withheld. Each Participating Organisation is  
       responsible and liable for all acts and omissions of its subcontractors as if they were those of  
       the Party itself.
```
